what_it_does: Enumerates and documents the company's six foundational type-infrastructure registries—node types, projections, contracts, JSON schemas, forms, and bindings—listing every entry, its fields, legibility metadata, notable design rules, and cross-registry relationships.
observations: Contains a complete inventory of 16 node types with kind, ports, config fields, volatile flags, and a legibility registry in nodes/_meta.py
Lists 12 projections with produced_by, embeds, stage, drift home, and legibility mappings in bindings/by_lens.py
Documents 13 contracts: C1–C8 core contracts plus C-CAP, C-PLAT, and nested sub-models in contracts/
Catalogs 13 JSON schema files under schemas/vi-vision/v1/ including archetype, decision, decision-card, graph, lanes, legibility, and others
Defines 4 form shapes (decision, log, prose, registry) that route corpus units to effort bands by shape
Lists 7 Instrument bindings (raw, grouped, by_lens, by_node_type, by_cascade, by_nucleation, by_separator, semantic, time-of-day) plus legibility registries
Includes a consolidated 'Notable & Surprising' section with 20 design points and a cross-registry connections section
Ends with a summary table comparing the six areas by entries, pattern, volatility, and seam
standouts: Self-registering file-discovered registries: adding a file in nodes/projections/forms auto-registers the entry with zero code edits
VOLATILE discipline: nodes that read live filesystem truth (codebase, model_of_tim) must set VOLATILE=True so the memo gate does not serve stale results forever
Contracts are the zero-import spine: every other module composes against contracts, but contracts import nothing
Decision state resolves from a decision_take MARK on an address, not from an authored state field; explanation_source is a live address, not stored prose
Portal node is reference-resolved (RESOLVE='reference'): the scheduler skips firing it and the runtime dereferences config.ref live
conventions: Registry-is-truth: data rows drive runtime, UI, tools, and instrument without per-type hardcoding
Legibility separation: every registry has a parallel {id: {name, is}} mapping so operators see human meaning, never machine ids
Schema-additive versioning: new edge kinds or address schemes widen the legal set without invalidating existing records (e.g., default edge kind 'data' for pre-v2 graphs)
Fail-loud validation: typed Literals, explicit drift homes, and loud failures on novel values or missing forms
Shape-based deterministic routing: forms recognize file shape (timestamps, headers, link density) rather than using AI content judgment
Neutral descriptive register: no ranking, complete enumeration, coverage-complete inventory
concerns: C6 (context-variable contracts) is flagged as planned but not yet implemented; C8/ui_info.py is described only as a large contract without details in this file
Several schema files (instrument, selector, spatial-material, zones, diagram) are marked 'TBD by inspection' rather than fully specified
The document mixes dense registry tables, design commentary, and a 20-item notable list, which may become hard to keep synchronized as registries grow
The 'complete inventory, zero filtering' claim will require ongoing discipline to avoid drift
notes: A dense, neutral catalog of the company's six foundational type registries and their interconnections, intended as the interpretive map for runtime, UI, capture, and instrument composition.
questions: Which loader modules actually perform file-discovery for nodes, projections, forms, and bindings?
How is the legibility registry automatically kept in sync when a new data row is added?
What are the exact semantics of the form 'stage' and 'policy' fields beyond the four examples shown?
How does the fork's decision_registry validate against and reference the 13 JSON schemas?
What process updates the last_updated field and ensures it stays accurate?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to provide a complete, neutral map of the typed substrate; the file matches this by enumerating all entries and cross-connections with no ranking or filtering.
apparent_themes: Separation of machine identifiers from human legibility
intention_signals: It is trying to make the entire type substrate explicit, discoverable, and legible so that runtime, UI, capture pipelines, and the Instrument can all compose against shared registries instead of embedding hardcoded knowledge.
novelty: Distinctive in treating node types, projections, forms, and bindings as file-discovered, self-registering registries with separate legibility layers, and in making contracts a zero-import spine that uses closed typed-Literal validation to fail loud.
contribution: Provides a single neutral inventory and design rationale for the foundational registries, capturing entry counts, field semantics, volatility rules, edge kinds, and cross-registry relationships that a fused system would need to preserve.
summary_for_embedding: A complete neutral inventory of the company's six foundational registries—nodes, projections, contracts, schemas, forms, and bindings—listing entries, fields, legibility metadata, notable design rules, and cross-registry connections.
intention_for_embedding: To document the typed substrate so runtime, UI, capture, and instrument can compose against shared, self-registering registries without hardcoding.
